message:
docs(fastapi-core): clarify execute_with_panic_isolation limitations

Add clear examples showing what panics ARE caught (in closure before
returning future) vs. NOT caught (inside async block during await).
This prevents user confusion about the method's capabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-28p","title":"Debug mode with enhanced error context","description":"Implement debug vs production mode for error responses. Production mode hides internal details, returns only user-safe error messages, no stack traces or file paths. Debug mode (opt-in) includes source location (file:line:function) in error responses, shows full validation error context, includes handler name and route pattern. Configuration via FastAPI::new().debug(true).debug_header(X-Debug-Token, secret). Test requirements: verify debug info hidden in production, shown when enabled, debug header auth works, no sensitive data leaks.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:26:57.049583465Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T05:55:08.915752945Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["developer-experience","errors","phase-1","ux"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2dh","title":"Route priority and ordering rules","description":"Define clear route matching priority:
1. Exact/static segments over parameters
2. Named params over wildcards
3. Earlier registration order as tiebreaker
- Document rules clearly
- Consistent with FastAPI behavior

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- /users/me matches before /users/{id}
- /files/{name} matches before /files/{*path}
- Priority documented in user guide
- No ambiguous matching

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Priority scenarios
- Unit: Tiebreaker ordering
- Integration: Complex route set","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:41:17.615602723Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:18:53.907872268Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:18:53.907808529Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive priority tests and module documentation - 6 new tests covering static>param>wildcard priority, conflict detection, and complex route sets","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-2","priority","routing"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2dv","title":"Structured logging design with request context","description":"Design structured logging infrastructure:
- Request context propagation (request_id, region_id, trace_id)
- Log macros that auto-include context fields  
- Log levels (trace, debug, info, warn, error)
- Timing instrumentation for spans
- Integration with asupersync's tracing
- JSON output format for production

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- log_info!, log_debug! etc macros auto-inject request_id
- Spans can be opened/closed with timing
- Logs are valid JSON with consistent schema
- Works with both sync and async contexts

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Log output format validation
- Unit: Context propagation through call stack
- Integration: Log correlation across async boundaries","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:38:54.804912967Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:38:41.394025896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:38:41.394025896Z","close_reason":"Completed structured logging design in crates/fastapi-core/src/logging.rs. Includes: LogLevel enum, LogEntry with JSON/compact formatters, Span/AutoSpan timing, LogConfig presets, log macros (log_trace/debug/info/warn/error!), comprehensive tests. Design documents JSON schema and integrates with asupersync context. Note: asupersync has pre-existing compilation errors blocking test runs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","infrastructure","logging","phase-1"]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2i0","title":"SecurityScopes auto-injection","description":"Implement SecurityScopes for scope checking:

STRUCTURE:
SecurityScopes {
    scopes: Vec<String>,     // All required scopes from dependency chain
    scope_str: String,       // scopes.join(' ')
}

AUTO-INJECTION:
When handler parameter is typed as SecurityScopes,
inject with aggregated scopes from:
1. Route-level Security dependencies
2. Parent dependencies with scopes
3. Preserving order

USAGE:
async fn get_admin(
    token: BearerToken,
    scopes: SecurityScopes,
) -> impl IntoResponse {
    // scopes.scopes = ['admin', 'users:read']
    // Verify token has required scopes
}

SCOPE AGGREGATION:
- Collect from parent dependencies
- own_scopes + parent_scopes
- Deduplicate while preserving order

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Scopes aggregated correctly
- Order preserved
- Works with nested Security deps
- scope_str formatted correctly

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Scope aggregation
- Unit: Order preservation
- Unit: Nested dependencies
- Integration: Full auth flow","notes":"Implemented SecurityScopes struct with scope aggregation, deduplication, merge, FromRequest extraction via request extensions, and SecurityScopesError. 19 tests passing. Clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:28.923582672Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:45.467890006Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:45.467761466Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["oauth2","phase-6","scopes","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-2i0","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-nij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2im","title":"Timing-safe comparison for auth tokens","description":"Provide constant-time comparison functions for sensitive values to prevent timing attacks on authentication.

Implementation:
- constant_time_compare() function
- Wrap subtle crate or implement carefully
- Use for password/token/HMAC comparison
- Document timing attack risks
- Integration with auth extractors

Acceptance Criteria:
- Comparison time independent of string content
- Works for any byte slices
- Clear documentation on usage
- Integration with token validators

Tests:
- Same strings compare equal
- Different strings compare unequal
- Timing measurement shows constant time
- Different length strings handled safely","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:14:28.030757625Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T08:19:17.241881147Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T08:19:17.241833047Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","crypto","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-2im","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-3d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2l1","title":"HTTP request body handling (Content-Length + chunked)","description":"Implement request body reading:
- Content-Length based reading with limits
- Chunked transfer encoding parsing
- Body streaming without full buffering
- Integration with asupersync checkpoints

DEPENDS ON: HTTP header parser

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Both Content-Length and chunked bodies work
- Body size limits enforced before reading
- Streaming API for large bodies
- Checkpoint calls during long reads

SECURITY:
- Reject bodies exceeding configured limit
- Timeout on slow clients (slowloris protection)

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Content-Length parsing
- Unit: Chunked encoding edge cases
- Unit: Size limit enforcement
- Security: Oversized body rejection
- Security: Slow client handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:16.409057432Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:34:59.346466795Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:34:59.346466795Z","close_reason":"Implemented body.rs module with Content-Length and chunked encoding support, size limits, and 25 passing tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["body","http","parser","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-2l1","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-k9h","title":"Request timeout via Budget","description":"Use asupersync's Budget for request timeouts:

## Budget Configuration
- Default budget per request (configurable)
- Budget propagates through handler and dependencies
- Remaining budget accessible via RequestContext.budget()

## Timeout Behavior
- Budget exhaustion triggers cancellation
- Handler receives CancelledError at next checkpoint
- Response: 504 Gateway Timeout with configurable body
- Clean resource cleanup via structured concurrency

## Integration Points
- TCP server creates budget for each request
- Middleware chain respects budget
- Extractors check budget before expensive operations
- Dependencies can subdivide budget

## Test Requirements
- Unit: Budget creation and propagation
- Unit: Timeout triggers cancellation
- Integration: Slow handler returns 504
- Integration: Cleanup runs on timeout","acceptance_criteria":"Slow handlers cancelled when budget exhausted, return 504.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-01-17T14:08:09.176106485Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:09:19.659784397Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:09:19.659738230Z","close_reason":"Implemented request timeout via Budget API:
- Added Cx::for_testing_with_budget(budget: Budget) to asupersync for creating Cx with custom budget
- Wired ServerConfig request_timeout to Budget deadline in server.rs
- Added timeout detection after handler completion using Instant elapsed time
- Returns 504 Gateway Timeout when request exceeds configured timeout
- Added 6 unit tests for timeout behavior (budget creation, duration conversion, 504 response)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cross-cutting","timeout"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-kl2","title":"Checkpoint integration in extractors and middleware","description":"Integrate cancellation checkpoints throughout the request pipeline for cancel-correctness. Checkpoint locations: before body read, after body parse, before each dependency resolve, before handler call, in middleware pre/post. Use ctx.checkpoint() to allow graceful cancellation. Masked sections: transaction commits, response send. Benefits: early exit on client disconnect, clean resource cleanup. Test: verify checkpoint returns CancelledError when cancelled, masked sections complete, resources cleaned up.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:45:43.727662665Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:39.003866055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:39.003810070Z","close_reason":"Completed checkpoint integration + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancel","extractors","middleware"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-kl2","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-3f1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"fastapi_rust-kpe","title":"Scope constraint validation","description":"Enforce dependency scope constraints:

RULE (per FastAPI spec):
Request-scoped dependency CANNOT depend on function-scoped dependency.
This would cause the request-scoped to hold stale function-scoped value.

EXAMPLE VIOLATION:
@depends(scope='request')
async fn get_cached_user(db: Depends<DbConn, scope='function'>) -> User
// ERROR: request scope can't depend on function scope

VALIDATION:
- Check at dependency graph construction
- Raise DependencyScopeError
- Clear message explaining the violation

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Scope violation detected at startup
- Clear error message
- Explain why it's invalid
- Allow valid scope combinations

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Invalid scope combination
- Unit: Valid scope combinations
- Unit: Nested scope validation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:12:18.297715683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T15:12:18.297715683Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["di","phase-4","safety"]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-lom","title":"Response timing metrics collection","description":"Collect and expose response timing metrics for monitoring. Track time-to-first-byte, total response time, etc.

Implementation:
- Request start time capture
- Time-to-first-byte (TTFB)
- Total response time
- Server-Timing header support
- Histogram collection
- Integration with logging

Metrics Collected:
- request_duration_ms: Total handler time
- ttfb_ms: Time to first byte
- body_write_ms: Time to write body
- db_time_ms: Database query time (via context)

Server-Timing Header:
- Exposes timing to browser DevTools
- Multiple metrics in one header
- Optional descriptions

Acceptance Criteria:
- Server-Timing header in responses
- Metrics available for logging/monitoring
- Sub-millisecond precision
- No significant performance overhead
- Integration with tracing

Tests:
- Server-Timing header present
- Timing values are reasonable
- Multiple timing entries work
- Disabled in production if needed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:28.467619364Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:28.467619364Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","metrics","observability","response"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-lom","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-e5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-lrz","title":"Dependency overrides for testing","description":"Implement dependency override system for testing:

API:
app.dependency_overrides[RealDbPool] = MockDbPool
app.dependency_overrides.insert::<RealDbPool>(mock_pool);

BEHAVIOR:
- When resolving Depends<RealDbPool>, use MockDbPool instead
- Override checked before normal resolution
- Supports any FromRequest type
- Clear overrides: app.dependency_overrides.clear()

USE IN TESTS:
#[test]
fn test_with_mock() {
    let mut app = create_app();
    app.override_dependency::<DbPool>(mock_pool);
    let client = TestClient::new(app);
    // Tests use mock_pool instead of real pool
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Override replaces real dependency
- Type-safe override API
- Works with nested dependencies
- Clear/reset functionality

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Override application
- Unit: Nested override
- Unit: Clear overrides
- Integration: Test client with overrides","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:11:43.912622237Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:25.344555165Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:25.344475676Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: DependencyOverrides provides insert(), insert_value(), clear(), and resolve() methods. Tests verify override substitution, nested resolution, complex graphs, and clear functionality. Integration with TestClient working.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["di","phase-4","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-lrz","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-aha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
